page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. The underlying CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute workloads. The instance achieves middle-tier results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, as well as in Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations. However, a significant performance bottleneck is observed in memory subsystem tests. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths fall into the bottom 25%, measuring 23.86 GB/sec and 23.08 GB/sec respectively, which is mirrored by a weak PassMark memory score of 1624.49. LLM inference performance is also highly constrained, dropping to the bottom 10% for medium 7B models, though it maintains average speeds for small 135M models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g7t.2xlarge offers a dedicated hardware profile that avoids resource contention, making it a stable choice for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without the premium of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. Given its balanced resource allocation and benchmark profile, this server is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations. It is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or large-scale machine learning inference due to its constrained large-block memory bandwidth and lack of hardware acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for standard compute tasks, including software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. However, it exhibits weak performance in large-block uncached memory read and write operations, as well as poor performance for medium-sized LLM inference. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without extreme memory or storage demands, making it suitable for general-purpose application hosting, web serving, and moderate development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering stable mid-tier compute performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.2xlarge features 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 32 GB RAM, offering balanced, mid-tier general-purpose compute performance.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core Intel Xeon instance offering balanced compute resources and baseline network performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Average performance in standard compute, compression, and web serving benchmarks
Constrained large-block memory bandwidth and limited LLM inference capabilities
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and software compilation
